content.problemStatement:
You want to buy NNN items from a store, which you will visit on Days 111, 222, ..., NNN. The normal cost of the iii-th item is AiA_iAi‚Äã.

However, on the iii-th day, there is a promotional event for the iii-th item. On this day, it's cost is BiB_iBi‚Äã instead. BiB_iBi‚Äã may be greater, smaller or even equal to AiA_iAi‚Äã.

Thus, on the iii-th day, the cost of the iii-th item is BiB_iBi‚Äã while, the jjj-th item (j‚âÝi)(j \ne i)(jÓÄÝ=i) costs AjA_jAj‚Äã.

You will buy **exactly one** item on each of the NNN days. All the bought items must be distinct. Find the minimum possible cost.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains NNN - the number of items
The second line contains A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,...,AN‚Äã - the normal costs of the items
The third line contains B1,B2,....,BNB_1, B_2, ...., B_NB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,....,BN‚Äã - the promotional costs of the items.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum cost.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The following is a possible optimal approach :

Buy Item 222 on Day 111 for cost of 222. (Normal Cost)
Buy Item 111 on Day 222 for cost of 111. (Normal Cost)
Buy Item 333 on Day 333 for cost of 111. (Promotional Cost)

The total cost is 2+1+1=42 + 1 + 1 = 42+1+1=4.
